trajectory.description: Observation: Starting fresh (no prior workspace). Analysis: Using CLIP ViT-B/16 (pre-cached, no network) + linear classifier for strong baseline on image classification; standard CLIP transforms; DataParallel across 4 GPUs; 2 epochs to establish quick baseline + measure iteration time per instructions. Decision: Implement from-scratch model/train/predict scripts, run short training, then submit_eval immediately for initial macro_f1 and timing.

[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'macro_f1': 0.1394, 'accuracy': 0.6725, 'macro_recall': 0.1485, 'n_eval_classes': 102}

trajectory.description: Observation: CLIP-B/16 linear probe baseline achieves macro_f1=0.1215 (acc=0.63) after 2 epochs; ~28min/epoch so ~5-10 epochs feasible in budget; no val monitoring or best-model selection (saves last only); logging sparse. Analysis: long-tail + OOD needs better macro_f1 via val-based checkpointing, class-balanced loss or aug; larger effective batch via higher bs + 4-GPU DP; modest LR/scheduler. Decision: increment to 6 epochs, add val loop every epoch computing macro_f1 (sklearn), save best checkpoint on val macro_f1, increase bs=96, add CosineAnnealingLR, minor aug tweak; submit_eval on new best to measure gain and time.

trajectory.description: Observation: CLIP-B/16 fine-tune (6eps) reached test macro_f1=0.1394 (acc=0.6725, n_classes=102) from 0.1215 baseline; val macro_f1~0.022 due to averaging over all 182 classes (many zero-support in val); ~17min/epoch so ~12eps feasible; curves show training but val selection flawed. Analysis: metric mismatch hurts best-ckpt (task uses unique(y_true) only); long-tail hurts rare classes (macro_f1); ViT-L/14 has more capacity; balanced sampling will boost rare species. Decision: switch to CLIP ViT-L/14, fix val_f1 with np.unique(all_labels), add WeightedRandomSampler (1/sqrt(freq)) for train, set 12eps/bs=128/T_max=12/lr=5e-5, save best on corrected val_f1, submit_eval.
